title: colchicum fharii subsp. gibraltaricum
intro: Colchicum fharii subsp. gibraltaricum is a rare and endemic subspecies of the autumn-flowering cormous perennial plant Colchicum, native to Gibraltar. The plant is also known as the "Gibraltar Autumn Crocus" and contains a toxic alkaloid colchicine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Colchicine has been used to treat gout and rheumatoid arthritis due to its ability to inhibit mitosis, thereby reducing inflammation and pain. Colchicum fharii subsp. gibraltaricum is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or powder for herbal remedies, but its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity.
